title: Give switcher and login their own bookmarkable URLs
action: Extend pathForRoute/route-parsing in web/src/main.ts so switcher gets /switcher and login gets /login as real paths, alongside the existing /terminal/<pane_id> (per D1) -- Route/pathForRoute must no longer collapse switcher and login to '/'. Add or generalize a path-parsing function (parseTerminalPaneId's narrower job can be folded in) that recognizes /login, /switcher, and /terminal/<id>; '/' and any other path are unrecognized (per D2). In bootstrap(), when the resolved path is '/' or unrecognized, silently canonicalize the URL via history.replaceState to whichever concrete route the existing fetchAgents() session probe resolves to -- /switcher if authenticated, /login if not -- mirroring PBI-025 D3's silent-fallback style (no toast/error). When the probe succeeds (authenticated) and the requested path was /login specifically, redirect straight to switcher instead of rendering the login form (D3). When the probe fails (unauthenticated) and the requested path was /switcher, /login, '/', or unrecognized, show the login screen and canonicalize the URL to /login via replaceState (D5) -- but when the requested path was a /terminal/<pane_id> link, keep showLogin()'s existing behavior of leaving the URL unchanged (D5's carve-out, preserving PBI-025 D5's intended-pane-across-login-round-trip). Give navigate() a narrow exception (per D7): any transition whose destination route is 'login', or that leaves the 'login' route (handleLoginSuccess's navigate call after a successful login), must use history.replaceState instead of history.pushState regardless of whether the path changed -- every other transition keeps navigate()'s existing push-on-path-change rule (D6). Do not add any history./location. calls to login.ts, switcher.ts, or terminal.ts -- all navigation stays routed through navigate()/goBack()/the single popstate listener in main.ts, per critical-patterns.md's standing rule. Update web/test/main.test.ts to match: the existing 'resolves login and switcher to root / (D4)' and 'replaces the current entry instead of pushing when the path is unchanged (switcher <-> login share /)' assertions describe pre-D1 behavior and must be rewritten to describe the new behavior, not left contradicting the code.

truths: Opening /switcher directly with a valid session renders switcher without visiting login first.
Opening /login directly with a valid session redirects straight to switcher; the login form is never shown to an already-authenticated session (D3).
Opening /switcher, /login, '/', or any unrecognized path without a valid session shows the login screen and canonicalizes the URL to /login via replaceState (D5).
Opening a /terminal/<pane_id> link without a valid session shows the login screen with the URL left unchanged, exactly as PBI-025 D5 already established -- unaffected by this feature.

After a successful login, pressing browser/phone Back never renders the login form again to the now-authenticated operator (D7) -- the transition into or out of 'login' always replaces the current history entry instead of pushing a new one.
/terminal/<pane_id> behavior (URL scheme, pushState/popstate, refresh recovery, login-redirect preservation) is unchanged from PBI-025 (D1-D5 of pbi-025-terminal-detail-url).

artifacts.substantive: pathForRoute/route-parsing extended for /switcher and /login as concrete paths; bootstrap()'s canonicalization and login-redirect logic cover D2-D5; navigate() carries D7's login-transition replace exception -- no stub/TODO logic

test coverage added/updated for: /switcher and /login path construction and parsing, authenticated-visits-/login redirect (D3), unauthenticated-visits-/switcher-or-root canonicalization to /login (D5), and the D7 replaceState-on-login-transition back-stack behavior -- the two now-stale D4-era assertions (root-collapse, switcher<->login-share-'/' replace) are rewritten, not left contradicting the new code; prior /terminal/<pane_id> assertions (PBI-025 D1-D5) still pass

key_links: navigate() checks whether the destination or origin route is 'login' before deciding push vs replace (D7), not just a path-string comparison
bootstrap()'s canonicalization branch and handleLoginSuccess both reuse the same session-probe/resolve logic already established by resolveLoginRedirect, generalized rather than duplicated
prohibitions: No server-side route changes (SPA fallback in src/web/mod.rs already returns index.html for /switcher and /login the same way it does for /terminal/<id>, confirmed in CONTEXT.md)
No change to LoginProps, SwitcherProps, or TerminalProps public signatures
No change to /terminal/<pane_id>'s own URL scheme, pushState/popstate, or login-redirect-preservation behavior (PBI-025 D1-D5 stay exactly as shipped)

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: pathForRoute/parseRoutePath extended so switcher and login get their own concrete /switcher and /login paths (D1); '/' is now unrecognized and canonicalized away (D2). bootstrap() drives all of D2-D5 through a new pure resolveBootstrapDecision(parsed, snapshot) helper: authenticated /login redirects straight to switcher (D3), unauthenticated /login|/switcher|'/'|unrecognized canonicalize to /login (D5), and the /terminal/<pane_id> carve-out (URL untouched on failed session probe) stays exactly as PBI-025 shipped it. navigate() gained D7's login-transition exception (checked via history.state.route, not a path-string compare): entering or leaving 'login' always replaceState, never pushState, so Back can never re-render the login form to an authenticated operator. No changes to login.ts/switcher.ts/terminal.ts or their Props types; both stale D4-era tests rewritten; new coverage added for parseRoutePath, resolveBootstrapDecision, and D7.

    "pathForRoute: rewrote the D4-era 'resolves login and switcher to root /' assertion into 'builds /login and /switcher for login and switcher routes (D1)'",
    "parseRoutePath: new describe block (4 cases) covering /login, /switcher, /terminal/<id> delegation, and null for '/'/unrecognized/malformed paths",
    "resolveBootstrapDecision: new describe block (6 cases) covering D3 (authenticated /login -> switcher), D2/D4 (authenticated /switcher|unrecognized -> switcher), D5 (unauthenticated /login|/switcher|unrecognized -> login, canonicalize), authenticated /terminal/<id> resolve and no-longer-resolves fallback, and the PBI-025 D5 carve-out (unauthenticated /terminal/<id> -> login, no canonicalize)",
    "navigate: rewrote the D4-era 'replaces when path unchanged (switcher<->login share /)' test into 3 D7 cases -- into-login always replaces, away-from-login always replaces, and a normal switcher<->terminal transition still pushes"

  "red_failure_evidence": "Pre-change web/src/main.ts (git show HEAD): pathForRoute() returned '/' for both {name:\"login\"} and {name:\"switcher\"} (D4-era), so pathForRoute({name:\"switcher\"})==='/login' and the new parseRoutePath('/switcher') tests would have failed against the old code (old pathForRoute gave no /switcher path at all, and there was no parseRoutePath/resolveBootstrapDecision export to import -- a TS compile failure). navigate()'s old body had no login-detection branch at all: pre-change, navigate({name:\"login\"}) from a differing path (e.g. '/terminal/w1:p1') would have called history.pushState (path differs, old pathForRoute(login)==='/'), which the new D7 test 'replaces instead of pushing when navigating into login, even from a different path' asserts must NOT happen -- confirmed by manually re-deriving the old function against that test's inputs.",

}
